title: Alphabet's investment book
question: Is Alphabet actually a skilled investor versus its peers?
finding: Best cost basis, no detectable picking skill. Alphabet got the cheapest megacap entry into two generational private winners (SpaceX, Anthropic; ~40–55× vs ~18× for Microsoft, ~9× for Amazon; +$24bn ≈ 18% of FY25 net income, but the same line lost money in 2022). On the public CapitalG/GV names I can actually price (22 of them vs a 1,078-name same-era IPO control), the backed basket shows no edge at any horizon once tested with a name-level bootstrap on the difference — and what looked like an edge is listing vintage: the control swings the same way.
